While smoking, hypertension, and elevated fibrinogen levels have well-documented strong correlations with increasing CAD risk—as illustrated notably by extensive epidemiologic studies and biological mechanisms—it becomes clearer that moderate alcohol consumption actually poses lesser absolute risks when balanced against these potent hazards. Moderate drinking might even confer certain cardioprotection benefits under strict regulatory limits, although heavy drinking indisputably amplifies CAD threats dramatically. Therefore, \( \boxed{D} \)'s inclusion reflects nuanced public health messaging recommending moderation rather than outright prohibition unlike stark warnings issued against smoking cessation targets.
